evidence_quotes.quote: videogames remain accessible offline even if online servers are shut down.
games that are bought by someone should be playable by someone.
It should be made illegal to disable videogames which have already been sold.

paragraphs.text: Dear representatives/lawmakers, I kindly suggest that the European Digital Fairness Act ensures that videogames remain accessible offline even if online servers are shut down. Like proposed in the 'Stop Destroying Videogames' initiative, games that are bought by someone should be playable by someone. Especially if it contains singleplayer modes that still require you to be online to play them, developers should have an end of life plan ready for the game, that when the servers are shut down, there is a plan to compensate players one way or another, which one way would be to keep the game playable. It should be made illegal to disable videogames which have already been sold.
